product_updates.title: Tesla just increased its spending plan to $25B — here’s where the money is going
product_updates.content: Tesla's planned capex for 2026 is three times higher than what the company has historically spent. Its CFO said, as a result, Tesla will have a negative free cash flow the rest of the year.

product_updates.title: Google updates Workspace to make AI your new office intern
product_updates.content: Google has introduced a host of new automated functions into Workspace, all of which are driven by Workspace Intelligence, its new AI system.

product_updates.title: Hands on with X’s new AI-powered custom feeds
product_updates.content: X's AI-powered custom timelines are replacing Communities, with Grok-curated feeds...and new ad slots.

product_updates.title: How SpaceX preempted a $2B fundraise with a $60B buyout offer
product_updates.content: Cursor was on track to close a $2 billion funding round this week but chose to halt discussions after SpaceX offered a $10 billion "collaboration fee" and a path to a $60 billion acquisition.

product_updates.title: Google Cloud launches two new AI chips to compete with Nvidia
product_updates.content: Google's newest TPUs are faster and cheaper than the previous versions. But the company is still embracing Nvidia in its cloud — for now.

product_updates.title: Google turns Chrome into an AI co-worker for the workplace
product_updates.content: Google brings Gemini-powered "auto browse" capabilities to Chrome for enterprise users, letting workers automate tasks like research, data entry, and more.

product_updates.title: Google makes an interesting choice with its new agent-building tool for enterprises
product_updates.content: Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform takes an interesting approach: It is geared for IT and technical users.

product_updates.title: AI Overviews are coming to your Gmail at work
product_updates.content: The AI Overviews will offer instant summaries pulled from across multiple emails.

product_updates.title: OpenAI teams up with Infosys to bring AI tools to more businesses
product_updates.content: Infosys said the integration will be used to help its clients modernize software development, automate workflows, and deploy AI systems, initially focusing software engineering, legacy modernization, and DevOps.

product_updates.title: AI is spitting out more potential drugs than ever. This startup wants to figure out which ones matter.
product_updates.content: 10x Science has raised a $4.8 million seed round to help pharmaceutical researchers understand complex molecules.

technical_breakthroughs.title: Microsoft issues emergency update for macOS and Linux ASP.NET threat
technical_breakthroughs.content: When authentication fails, things can go very, very wrong.

technical_breakthroughs.title: The Download: introducing the 10 Things That Matter in AI Right Now
technical_breakthroughs.content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Introducing: 10 Things That Matter in AI Right Now What actually matters in AI right now? It’s getting harder to tell amid the constant launches, hype, and war…

technical_breakthroughs.title: AI needs a strong data fabric to deliver business value
technical_breakthroughs.content: Artificial intelligence is moving quickly in the enterprise, from experimentation to everyday use. Organizations are deploying copilots, agents, and predictive systems across finance, supply chains, human resources, and customer operations. By the end of 2025, half of companies used AI in at leas…

technical_breakthroughs.title: 3 things Michelle Kim is into right now
technical_breakthroughs.content: Isegye Idol If you thought K-pop was weird, virtual idols—humans who perform as anime-style digital characters via motion capture—will blow your mind. My favorite is a girl group called Isegye Idol, created by Woowakgood, a Korean VTuber (a streamer who likewise performs as a digital persona). Is…

technical_breakthroughs.title: One town’s scheme to get rid of its geese
technical_breakthroughs.content: “Pull over!” I order my brother one sunny February afternoon. Our target is in sight: a gaggle of Canada geese, pecking at grass near the dog park. As I approach, tiptoeing over their grayish-white poop, I notice that one bird wears a white cuff around its slender black neck. It’s a GPS tracker—p…

technical_breakthroughs.title: There is no nature anymore
technical_breakthroughs.content: When people talk about “nature,” they’re generally talking about things that aren’t made by human beings. Rocks. Reefs. Red wolves. But while there is plenty of God’s creation to go around, it is hard to think of anything on Earth that human hands haven’t affected. In the Brazilian rainforest, sc…

technical_breakthroughs.title: Los Angeles is finally going underground
technical_breakthroughs.content: Los Angeles deserves its reputation as the quintessential car city—the rhythms of its 2,200 square miles are dictated by wide boulevards and concrete arcs of freeways. But it once had a world-class rail transit system, and for the last three decades, the city has been rebuilding a network of trol…
